title: Child Development Psychology Foundations
description:
Unlock the science behind child development with this engaging online course. Designed as a preview of the University of Surrey’s MSc Psychology program, this course introduces essential psychological theories and research, helping you determine if the full program aligns with your goals. 
 Why Take This Course? 
- Explore Key Psychological Theories – Understand attachment theory, theories of moral development, and emotion understanding. 
- Gain Real-World Insights – Learn how psychological principles apply to education, parenting, and policy. 
- Understand Children’s Rights – Investigate how children perceive their rights and how global policies shape their well-being. 
- Examine Emotional Intelligence – Explore how children develop emotion regulation and social awareness across cultures. 
- Develop Critical Thinking – Compare competing theories and reflect on how they shape modern psychology. 
- Flexible & Engaging – Learn at your own pace with videos, quizzes, and interactive activities. 
 Who Is This Course For? 
- Aspiring psychologists exploring child development and cognition. 
- Educators, caregivers, and professionals working with children. 
- Students considering the MSc Psychology at the University of Surrey. 
- Whether you’re looking to deepen your understanding of developmental psychology or considering a full MSc program, this course provides valuable knowledge that can be applied across multiple disciplines.
